latest.title: FiberPass Transaction Completes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6651V
Zegona Communications PLC
06 March 2026
6 March 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
FiberPass Transaction Completes
Zegona is pleased to announce
that on 5 March 2026, AXA's1 acquisition of a 40% stake in FiberPass2 completed
.
Vodafone Spain has
generated up front proceeds of ?0.4bn from this transaction. The
ownership of FiberPass is now 55% Telef
ó
nica, 40% AXA and 5% Vodafone Spain.
?0.2
bn of the proceeds will be used to fund the share buyback programme announced on 27 November 2025.
The remaining
?0.2
bn will be used for debt reduction and puts the business in a good position to deliver net debt of only
?3.2bn by the end of our financial year, March 2026.
This reinforces Zegona's commitment to its leverage target of 1.5X - 2X and further accelerates progress in reducing total annual interest costs3.
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented, "The AXA investment completes our transformation of Vodafone Spain's fixed network strategy. This transaction delivers significant proceeds which help drive returns to Zegona shareholders through our share buyback programme, a step change in net debt reduction and an acceleration in reducing total annual interest costs."
For further information, please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns.
?
Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
?
About Vodafone Spain
Vodafone Spain is a national provider of fixed, mobile and TV services in Spain, serving consumer, business and public administration customers. It was acquired by Zegona in 2024.
About Telef
ó
nica
Telefónica is one of the world's leading telecommunications service providers. The company offers fixed and mobile connectivity as well as a wide range of digital services for residential and business customers. With over 350 million customers, Telefónica operates in Europe and Latin America. Telefónica is listed on the Spanish stock market, New York and Lima.
Notes
1. AXA means BNPP AM Alts,
part of the BNP Paribas Group since 1st July 2025
2. FiberPass is
Compañía Mayorista de Fibra, S.L.,
the fibre joint venture between Vodafone Spain, Telefonica and BNPP AM Alts
3. Applying current market debt yields to the reduced net debt balance demonstrates the potential to drive annual interest costs well below
?200m
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0263V
Zegona Communications PLC
03 March 2026
3 March 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Appointment of Independent
Non-Executive Director
Zegona
is pleased to announce the appointment of Tim Pennington as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective immediately. He will join the Audit Committee upon appointment and will become Chair of the Audit Committee after the end of this financial year.
T
im is an experienced finance executive with a strong background in corporate finance, telecommunications and mergers and acquisitions.
He currently holds non-executive roles with MTN
Group
Limited
, Africa
's largest
mobile
network
operator
and one of its listed subsidiaries
.
Tim has
held senior financ
e
leadership roles at major
international
telecommunications companies, including Chief Financial Officer of Millicom
International Cellular S.A.
and
Cable & Wireless Communications plc, and
he
was
Group Finance Director for Cable & Wireless
PLC
. Earlier in his career, Tim served as Chief Financial Officer of Hutchison Telecom International and Finance Director at Hutchison 3G (UK), the British mobile business of Hutchison Whampoa. He also bri
ngs investment banking experience from his time as a director at Samuel Montagu & Co. Limited and as Managing Director at HSBC Investment Bank within its Corporate Finance and Advisory Department.
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented: "On behalf of the Board, I am delighted to welcome Tim to Zegona. His deep understanding of corporate finance and the global telecoms industry will be a great asset to our Board as we continue to execute our strategy and drive value creation."
For further information please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.?
This announcement is made pursuant to UKLR 6.4.8. The Company confirms that there are no additional matters requiring disclosure under UKLR 6.4.8.
At the date of this announcement, Tim Pennington has no beneficial interest in the ordinary shares of Zegona.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona builds and operates high‑speed fibre networks that provide broadband, cloud connectivity and managed network services to businesses and households across the UK.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits on a growing demand for ultra‑fast internet and low‑latency links driven by remote work, streaming, and 5G roll‑out. Its network covers key urban and rural corridors that many competitors still lack, giving it a competitive edge in a market that is being forced to upgrade infrastructure.
company_30s.current_state: Zegona is in a restructuring phase after a major fibre acquisition (FiberPass) and a share buyback. It has just been added to the MSCI UK Index, signalling increased institutional interest, and it is paying a dividend in early January. Cash burn remains moderate and the company is working to turn the new network capacity into recurring revenue.

rns_narrative: Corporate governance is being re‑aligned – a new directorate, updated voting rights and holdings have been announced, indicating a consolidation of ownership.
Operational milestones include the completion of the FiberPass transaction and a share‑buyback that should reduce dilution and improve earnings per share.
Market visibility has risen with inclusion in the MSCI UK Index and updated TLB reporting, giving the stock a broader institutional footprint.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious optimism – investors are waiting for a clear turnaround after the dividend.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the recent fibre deal, the dividend payout and the MSCI listing as signs that the company is on a path to higher earnings and that the market is starting to see the value of its network assets.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry about the debt load from the fibre transaction, the risk of falling behind competitors, and the possibility that the dividend will drain cash needed for future network expansion.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been largely flat but has edged bullish after the dividend announcement and the FiberPass completion; the community remains on the sidelines, waiting for a decisive catalyst.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no public interest spikes, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The ex‑dividend date and the subsequent payout may trigger a rebound as shareholders reinvest the cash.
Operational launch of the FiberPass network is expected to unlock new revenue streams and could push the price higher.
Potential regulatory announcements around broadband or 5G infrastructure could lift valuations for firms with a strong fibre footprint.
expect_next.watch_for: A surge in volume around the ex‑dividend, a clear move above the 200‑day moving average, and any new partnership or customer win that expands network usage.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of the crash‑recovery cycle. A bounce is likely after the ex‑dividend and the FibrePass rollout, but patience is required.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (over 80% down) sets the stage for a high‑reward rebound. Historical RED signals have delivered around 160% upside on average. If the price recovers to roughly halfway between the 52‑week low and high, that’s a 200% jump from the current level – a classic mean‑reversion play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The risk is that the fibre upgrade costs could outpace revenue and the company may struggle to service debt. If the dividend payout drains cash and the network doesn’t generate expected traffic, the stock could remain stalled or fall to a new low. Worst‑case: a 20% drop from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 2–3× the current price versus a downside of only 10–20% if the market corrects. The asymmetry is strongly in favor of the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the ex‑dividend when volume spikes and the price closes above the 200‑day MA. Size the position for 10% of discretionary capital, set a stop 5% below entry, and hold for 3–4 months to ride the network rollout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike (at least twice the average daily volume) and a bounce above the 50‑day MA. Also look for a press release confirming new customer wins or a partnership that will increase traffic.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting, bankruptcy or fraud.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash, a dividend payout and the FibrePass launch create a high‑reward contrarian play with a favorable risk/reward profile.
